candidates.title: Equilibrium-Driven Weight Decay (EqWD) with Cumulative Alignment Contraction Theory
candidates.summary: Uses gradient-to-weight ratio deviation from layer-specific equilibrium as WD scheduling signal. Bridges Sun et al. (CVPR 2025) alignment theory with Defazio (2025) ratio dynamics. Layer-type-aware formulation for normalized vs. non-normalized layers. Paired with Cumulative Alignment Contraction bound (average-case generalization) and unified Phi framework.
hypotheses: H1: Cumulative contraction index correlates with generalization (r < -0.7)
H2: EqWD achieves >= 0.2% over SWD on CIFAR-100, >= 0.3% on ImageNet
H3: Alignment signal contains incremental information beyond norms
H4: Layer-type-aware WD matches or exceeds uniform alignment-aware WD
H5: Ratio equilibrium predictions match empirical values within 20%
candidates.pilot_focus: CIFAR-10/VGG-16-BN: validate ratio deviation signal variance and EqWD accuracy vs. fixed WD/SWD/CWD
risks: Ratio deviation too smooth at ImageNet scale

candidates.title: BCM Sliding Threshold Weight Decay (BCM-WD)
candidates.summary: Biologically-grounded dynamic WD using BCM sliding threshold mechanism. Alignment signal modulates WD relative to a slow EMA of historical alignment squared. Provides homeostatic stability guarantee from BCM convergence theory. Different mechanism from EqWD: modulates based on alignment surprise, not ratio equilibrium deviation.
hypotheses: BCM-WD outperforms frozen-threshold adaptive WD (sliding mechanism is active ingredient)
BCM-WD achieves comparable or better accuracy than CWD with fewer hyperparameters
candidates.pilot_focus: CIFAR-10/ResNet-20: BCM-WD vs. BCM-WD-frozen vs. CWD (diagnostic)
risks: WD direction conflicts with CWD convention
BCM stability conditions may not transfer to deep nets

candidates.title: Budget-Equivalent Evaluation Framework for Dynamic Weight Decay
candidates.summary: Purely empirical contribution: first rigorous budget-equivalent comparison of all WD streams using matched Bayesian optimization budgets. Standardized metrics (BEM, CSI, AIS). No new algorithm required. Paper focuses on answering 'Does dynamic WD actually help?' with definitive empirical evidence.
hypotheses: H6: At least one dynamic WD method beats tuned fixed WD at matched budget
Standardized metrics produce consistent rankings across architectures
candidates.pilot_focus: CIFAR pilot with 3 fixed WD values + CWD; check if accuracy gap is within noise
risks: Survey/benchmark papers face reviewer skepticism without novel algorithm

key_conflicts_resolved: Innovator/Pragmatist vs. Contrarian on alignment signal reliability -> Layer-type-aware formulation
BCM decay direction vs. CWD direction -> Include both in ablation
Empiricist null hypothesis vs. algorithmic contribution -> Budget equivalence as core experiment
